sprint: Governing Rules Compliance Pass — Single-Place Geometry + Frame-Editable Guardrail
task: A direct, evidence-based follow-up to the Guardrails sprint: after it shipped, the user set three permanent governing rules -- (1) Scene shows up in Creator exactly as it was in Builder, (2) every object on Scene honors the guardrails, (3) Creator can further refine objects through the right panel -- and asked for a compliance audit. Found and fixed two real, confirmed violations. Violation 1 (Rule 1): a single authored Place (no Dual-Holder layout) had its Builder-authored fractional position/size silently discarded in favour of the full panel rect, a deliberate mid-implementation decision from the Multiple Places sprint that no longer holds under Rule 1. Fixed in renderer/slideRenderer.js: _place1Rect and getPlaceRects(s) now subdivide Place 1 by its own authored fraction whenever ANY real placeRects array exists (length>=1); the full-panel fallback applies only when a Layout authors zero Places at all (no placeRects array -- the true legacy case, e.g. Museum Gallery, unaffected). Violation 2 (Rules 2/3): the 'Can a Story Author change this? (its Frame, once populated)' guardrail (holder.permissions.editable) had zero effect in Creator -- Card Designer's Frame Look/Frame Style controls stayed fully interactive regardless. Fixed with a new _currentPlaceEditable() helper reading SlideRenderer.getPlacePermissions(), wired into _refreshFrameWorkspace() (disables Frame Look buttons + shows a lock note) and _refreshBorder() (folds !editable into the existing !hasImage-disables pattern for every Frame Style control). A deliberate correction to the render-time default: _resolvePlacePermissions' editable field now defaults absent->true (not false like moveable), since Frame-editing is a PRE-EXISTING capability that has always been unrestricted -- defaulting it locked would have newly broken Frame-editing for every theme published before the Guardrails sprint, a real regression Rule 3 forbids. Verified via a re-run of the compliance audit (both violations now PASS, the World-owned-Decoration regression check still PASSes) plus full regression across the existing 28-script Creator/Builder Playwright suite, the Guardrails sprint's own 7-assertion suite, and both goldenBuild.js suites, all passing unchanged, zero console errors.
